docs.title: ShowControl Operator Manual
docs.text:
This manual is organized around the three core operator tasks:

  1. Installation / connection
  2. Using Setlists
  3. Using User-defined controls
  4. Using Ableset

docs.title: Recommended reading order
docs.text:
• First setup: Installation / connection
  • Show preparation: Using setlists
  • Performance control setup: Using user-defined controls

docs.title: Supporting pages
docs.text:
• Quick Start (Performer)
  • Show Day Checklist
  • Using Ableset
  • Troubleshooting
  • Style Guide

docs.title: ShowControl Manual Style Guide
docs.text:
This guide defines how to write and maintain user documentation for ShowControl in a live-performance context.

Scope: - Hardware controller - Desktop application - Ableton/script dependency - Optional Ableset integration

docs.title: 1) Audience model
docs.text:
Every page must be written for one primary audience:

  • Performer: fast, minimal, action-first
  • Operator/Tech: precise, diagnostic, system-level

If both are needed, split content into separate sections or pages.

docs.title: 2) Page structure rules
docs.text:
Preferred section order:

  1. Goal
  2. Preconditions
  3. Steps
  4. Expected result
  5. Failure path (link to troubleshooting)

For reference pages, use:

  1. Purpose
  2. Inputs/Outputs
  3. Limits/Defaults
  4. Operational notes

docs.title: 3) Responsibility boundaries (mandatory)
docs.text:
When documenting a feature, explicitly state ownership:

  • Hardware
  • ShowControl app
  • Ableton/script
  • Ableset

Avoid vague phrasing like “the system does X” without naming the layer.

docs.title: 4) Terminology (strict)
docs.text:
Use these canonical terms only:

  • Song: setlist song entry
  • Section: section inside a Song
  • User page: one configurable control page
  • Scenes/Markers source: left source list in Setlist

Do not alternate with synonyms inside docs.

docs.title: 5) Writing style
docs.text:
Target style: technical/operator oriented.

  • concise, direct, imperative
  • no marketing language
  • no ambiguous words (“maybe”, “probably”) in procedures
  • short paragraphs and dense signal

Good: - “Set IP and port, then press Send.”

Avoid: - “You might want to check network settings if needed.”

docs.title: 6) Formatting standards
docs.text:
• Use ## for major sections, ### for substeps
  • Use checklists for pre-show/showday tasks
  • Use compact tables for mapping/reference data
  • Use horizontal rules (---) between major blocks
  • Keep one task per numbered step

For in-app docs navigation: - subsection labels must match heading text exactly where possible

docs.title: 7) Operational safety content
docs.text:
Any live-critical page should include:

  • What to verify before show
  • What not to change mid-show
  • Fallback sequence
  • Recovery order

If a section can break show flow, add an explicit warning note.

docs.title: 8) Troubleshooting design
docs.text:
Troubleshooting must be symptom-first:

  • “Controller not detected”
  • “No Scenes/Markers shown”
  • “Wrong Song/Section triggered”

Each symptom entry should include:

  1. Fast checks (<= 60s)
  2. Deep checks
  3. Escalation/restart order

docs.title: 9) Versioning and change control
docs.text:
For every doc update:

  • keep terminology stable
  • update submenu anchors if headings changed
  • verify links in Info tab navigation
  • commit with explicit scope (docs(...))

When large rewrites happen: - keep a dated backup snapshot of prior docs

docs.title: 10) Quality checklist before publish
docs.text:
• [ ] Audience is clear
  • [ ] Preconditions are explicit
  • [ ] Steps are executable as written
  • [ ] Expected results are measurable
  • [ ] Failure path is present
  • [ ] Terminology is consistent
  • [ ] Submenu anchors match headings
  • [ ] No stale defaults/values

docs.title: 11) Recommended page set
docs.text:
Minimum operational doc set:

  • index.md (entry + scope)
  • quickstart.md (performer path)
  • showday-checklist.md (operator checklist)
  • application.md (UI workflows)
  • hardware.md (physical + IO)
  • ableset.md (integration patterns)
  • troubleshooting.md (symptom-first)
  • STYLEGUIDE.md (this document)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Define how ShowControl is operated when Ableset participates in the control/playback chain.

docs.title: Responsibility split
docs.text:
• ShowControl hardware: physical control surface input
  • ShowControl app: mappings, setlist UI, operational state
  • Ableton/script: musical timing and source context
  • Ableset: orchestration and external show logic

docs.title: Operating patterns
docs.text: Pattern Primary logic owner Notes Ableton-first Ableton locators/markers Use Scenes/Markers source in Setlist tab Ableset-first Ableset orchestration Keep ShowControl as physical operator layer

docs.title: Preconditions
docs.text:
• Network topology known and stable
  • Correct app/script instances loaded
  • Setlist ownership decided (Ableton-first vs Ableset-first)

docs.title: Validation sequence
docs.text:
1. Confirm connection path (USB/ETH).
  2. Confirm Song order and Section navigation.
  3. Validate Shift-layer controls.
  4. Validate deterministic jump behavior.
  5. Validate fallback behavior for path loss.

Expected result - Operator can move Song/Section state predictably under load.

If not, go to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: Desktop Application
docs.text:
The ShowControl desktop app has five main tabs:

  • Info
  • Setlist
  • Performance
  • User
  • Settings

The typical workflow is:

  1. Configure device/network in Settings
  2. Build and verify content in Setlist
  3. Validate mappings in User
  4. Use Performance during rehearsal/show

docs.title: Main navigation
docs.text:
The top bar is your global control area:

  • main tab switching
  • USB status indicator
  • Ethernet status indicator

docs.title: What to verify first
docs.text:
• at least one active connection path (USB or ETH)
  • expected tab selected for your current task

docs.title: Setlist tab
docs.text: Setlist is the primary preparation workspace.

docs.title: Main areas
docs.text:
• Left column: source content (Scenes/Markers)
  • Right column: Songs/Sections list
  • Top controls:
  • undo/redo
  • setlist selector
  • new/rename/duplicate/delete setlist
  • total setlist duration

docs.title: Typical setlist workflow
docs.text:
1. Choose source (Scenes or Markers)
  2. Create or select target setlist
  3. Drag items into Songs
  4. Organize Sections within each Song
  5. Reorder for final show flow
  6. Validate duration and transitions

docs.title: Good practice
docs.text:
• keep Song names performance-friendly
  • keep Sections meaningful for jumps/loops
  • use undo/redo often during arrangement

docs.title: Performance tab
docs.text:
Performance is a focused live-use view.

Use it for fast access during rehearsal and show operation when you no longer need deep editing.

docs.title: User tab
docs.text: User tab defines controller behavior for each User page.

docs.title: You can configure
docs.text:
• button actions (normal + shifted)
  • encoder turn/push actions
  • pedal behavior
  • display line content

docs.title: Recommended validation pass
docs.text:
For each User page used in your show:

  1. Test critical transport actions
  2. Test next/previous Song behavior
  3. Test shifted actions
  4. Test pedals and encoder

docs.title: Settings tab
docs.text: Settings combines visual hardware settings and network communication settings.

docs.title: Device settings
docs.text:
• LED brightness
  • display brightness

docs.title: Network settings
docs.text:
• IP address (4 fields)
  • port
  • send/apply action

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• keep production network values documented
  • avoid changing network values during a live section

docs.title: Info tab
docs.text:
Info is the built-in documentation viewer.

  • left column: manual navigation
  • right pane: current page content
  • version label: quick app version reference

docs.title: Best-practice flow before a show
docs.text:
1. Confirm USB/ETH status
  2. Confirm Settings values
  3. Finalize setlist order and durations
  4. Validate User page mappings
  5. Rehearse key transitions in Performance

docs.title: Installing and Connecting the ShowControl Controller
Ableton Live Control Surface Script
docs.text:
1. Unzip the script archive.
  2. Copy the folder into Ableton Live’s Remote Scripts directory (usually found in the installation folder or your user library).
  3. Restart Ableton Live to detect the new script.

docs.title: ShowControl Application
docs.text:
1. Download the .dmg file for the ShowControl app.
  2. Open the file and follow the instructions to install the application on your computer.
  3. Launch the app after installation.

docs.title: USB Connection
docs.text:
• Connect the USB cable between the controller and your computer.You will see USB 🟢 on the top right of the application.
  • Open Ableton Live, go to Preferences, and add a Control Surface line: select “Show Control” and set the MIDI In and Out columns to “Show Control DAW”.
  • The controller will display a white SHCTRL 1 icon, indicating an active USB connection.

Required for USB connection:

ShowControl Control Surface script set in Live, with In and Out set to Show Control DAW port

docs.title: Ethernet Connection
docs.text:
• Connect the Ethernet cable between the controller and your network or directly to your computer.
  • You can power the controller either by connecting the USB-C port to a charger or computer, or by using a Power over Ethernet (PoE) injector or switch on your Ethernet cable.
  • On the controller, press and hold the encoder to access the Settings menu.
  • Set the IP address according to your network requirements (make sure the first three digits match your computer’s address). e.g. : 192.168.10.12
  • On your computer, set the Ethernet port’s IPv4 address manually, using an address in the same subnet as the controller. e.g. : 192.168.10.10

[CAPTURE d'ECRAN MANUAL IP MACOS]

  • Launch the ShowControl app: it will automatically detect your device and establish the connection. You will see ETH 🟢 on the top right of the application.
  • The SHCTRL icon on the controller will turn green and the number 1 will appear, indicating an active network connection.
  • Make sure the Show Control script is loaded in Ableton Live so the app can communicate with Live.

Required for Ethernet connection:

  • ShowControl Control Surface script set in Live
  • ShowControl application running
  • Controller and computer in the same subnet.

docs.title: External MIDI
docs.text: You can connect a USB compliant MIDI device on the USB-A port. All the MIDI messages will be forwarded to the Show Control (External) MIDI port. If running the controller via Ethernet, a Virtual MIDI port will be created on which the MIDI messages will be forwarded.

docs.title: Pedals
docs.text: You can plug 2 footswitch pedals to perform actions.

docs.title: If Installation or Connection Fails
docs.text:
See the Troubleshooting section for detailed solutions.

Requirements

  • ShowControl controller and computer available
  • ShowControl app installed and working
  • Ableton Live project and script available (if using Ableton integration)
  • Required cables (USB-C, Ethernet, power)

docs.title: Hardware Controller
docs.text: This page covers physical setup, on-stage usage, and quick diagnostics.

docs.title: Power
docs.text:
Controller power options:

  • USB-C
  • PoE (if available on your hardware path)

If both are connected, PoE may be prioritized depending on your hardware implementation.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
• use stable, tested power supplies
  • avoid mid-show power path changes

docs.title: Connection modes
docs.text: Service Connection ShowControl App Ableton Script Ableset ShowControl USB Optional Recommended Optional ShowControl Ethernet Recommended Recommended Optional Ableset workflow USB Recommended Optional Required Ableset workflow Ethernet Optional Optional Required

docs.title: MIDI ports
docs.text:
When connected by USB, expected ports are:

  • Show Control DAW
  • Show Control External MIDI

If these are missing, check cable/port and reconnect sequence.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Long encoder press: open menu/navigation
  • Short encoder press: validate/select
  • Shift/Lock: alternate actions / exit actions

docs.title: Setlist / Ableset page
docs.text:
Used for Song and Section navigation in live context.

Typical operations:

  • previous/next Song
  • section changes
  • transport-oriented actions (depending on mapping/service mode)

docs.title: User pages
docs.text:
Up to 4 User pages are available for custom control layouts.

Each User page can combine:

  • primary + shifted button actions
  • encoder mappings
  • pedal mappings
  • display lines

docs.title: Settings page
docs.text:
Hardware-side settings usually include:

  • LED brightness
  • display brightness
  • network IP/port
  • device ID

Save values before exiting when applicable.

docs.title: Pedals
docs.text:
Supported pedal modes:

  • footswitch
  • expression
  • dual

Verify pedal mode and wiring before show day.

docs.title: External MIDI
docs.text:
External USB MIDI devices can be connected and forwarded through the external MIDI path.

Use powered hubs for higher current devices.

docs.title: Quick pre-show hardware check
docs.text:
• power stable
  • expected MIDI ports visible
  • network link valid (if ETH)
  • pedals/encoder responding

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Bring the rig to a stable, show-ready state with validated control and navigation.

docs.title: Preconditions
docs.text:
• Controller connected (USB and/or Ethernet)
  • ShowControl app running on operator machine
  • If using Ableton integration: set is open and script is loaded

docs.title: 1) Connect and validate transport path
docs.text:
1. Connect controller via USB or Ethernet.
  2. Open ShowControl app.
  3. Confirm at least one active link indicator (USB or ETH).

Expected result - Controller is reachable and status path is visible.

If not, go to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: 2) Validate device/network settings
docs.text:
1. Open Settings tab.
  2. Set LED/display brightness for venue conditions.
  3. If using Ethernet: verify IP + port, then press Send.

Expected result - Brightness and network values apply without errors.

If not, go to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: 3) Build or verify setlist structure
docs.text:
1. Open Setlist tab.
  2. Select source (Scenes or Markers).
  3. Create/select setlist.
  4. Arrange Songs and Sections.
  5. Confirm order and transitions.

Expected result - Setlist matches show order and is navigation-safe.

If not, go to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: 4) Validate control mappings
docs.text:
1. Open User tab.
  2. Validate critical actions per active User page:
  3. transport actions
  4. previous/next Song
  5. Shift layer
  6. encoder and pedals

Expected result - Critical actions are deterministic and repeatable.

If not, go to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: 5) Final pre-show gate
docs.text:
• [ ] Correct setlist selected
  • [ ] First Song selected
  • [ ] Shift state confirmed
  • [ ] Connection path stable
  • [ ] Critical controls re-tested

If any item fails, stop and resolve before starting playback.

docs.title: Show Day Checklist
docs.text: Operator-first checklist for rehearsal and live runtime.

docs.title: Pre-show (T-30 to T-10)
docs.text:
• [ ] Controller powered and physically secured
  • [ ] USB/ETH route validated
  • [ ] App running on correct machine/profile
  • [ ] Correct setlist loaded
  • [ ] First Song selected
  • [ ] Critical mappings validated (transport, Song nav, Shift)

docs.title: Line-check (T-10 to T-2)
docs.text:
• [ ] Play/Stop test
  • [ ] Previous/Next Song test
  • [ ] Section jump test
  • [ ] Shift-layer test
  • [ ] Pedal + encoder test

docs.title: During show (operator rules)
docs.text:
• Do not change IP/port mid-song.
  • Do not remap controls mid-song unless emergency.
  • Keep fallback path ready before each transition.

docs.title: Fallback protocol
docs.text:
1. Verify active transport path (USB/ETH indicator).
  2. Verify selected setlist and current Song.
  3. Execute known-safe action (e.g., Play/Stop check).
  4. If unstable, restart app at safe transition point.
  5. Re-validate first actionable control before resuming.

docs.title: Post-show capture
docs.text:
• What failed?
  • What took too long?
  • Which action needs a dedicated control?
  • Which mapping should be simplified?

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom-first diagnostics for operator use.

docs.title: Fast checks
docs.text:
1. Use a data USB cable.
  2. Try another USB port.
  3. Restart controller + app.
  4. Confirm expected MIDI ports appear.

docs.title: Deep checks
docs.text:
• isolate to minimal rig (controller + single cable + app)
  • check for competing MIDI clients

docs.title: Escalation
docs.text: • reboot operator machine if port enumeration is inconsistent

docs.title: Fast checks
docs.text:
1. Confirm Ableton set is open.
  2. Confirm remote script is loaded.
  3. Reconnect after Ableton is fully ready.

docs.title: Deep checks
docs.text:
• verify correct project context and expected markers/scenes exist
  • verify app path is using intended source mode

docs.title: Fast checks
docs.text:
1. Verify Song order in Setlist tab.
  2. Verify Section placement inside each Song.
  3. Check for misplaced drops/duplicates.
  4. Use undo/redo to inspect latest operations.

docs.title: Deep checks
docs.text:
• validate jump logic against rehearsal sequence
  • retest with simplified setlist slice

docs.title: Fast checks
docs.text:
1. Confirm active User page.
  2. Confirm Shift state.
  3. Re-check assignment values.

docs.title: Deep checks
docs.text:
• test one control at a time on isolated target action
  • remove overlapping mappings during diagnosis

docs.title: Fast checks
docs.text:
1. Verify IP + port on both ends.
  2. Verify same subnet.
  3. Verify firewall policy.

docs.title: Deep checks
docs.text:
• test direct link
  • test with reduced network load

docs.title: Recovery order (safe)
docs.text:
1. Save state if possible
  2. Restart app
  3. Power cycle controller
  4. Re-validate first Song + critical controls
  5. Resume from known-safe transition point

docs.title: Building the Setlist
docs.text:
1. In the application, open the Setlist tab.
  2. Select source (Scenes or Markers).
  3. Create/select target setlist.
  4. Drag content into the Songs box.
  5. Organize Sections inside Songs.

[CAPTURE d'ECRAN SETLIST]

docs.title: Setlists
docs.text:
• You can rename, duplicate, remove, create Setlists.
  • The selected Setlist is the currently active Setlist.
  • When opening a set, the last selected Setlist is active by default.

docs.title: Songs
docs.text:
• You can reorder Songs by drag and drop.
  • You can Rename a Song by right clicking or double clicking it.
  • You can Remove a Song by right clicking or dropping it outside the Songs box.

docs.title: Sections
docs.text:
• You can reorder Sections by drag and drop.
  • You can Remove a Section by right clicking or dropping it. outside the Songs box.

docs.title: Session Playback
docs.text:
• For Session Scenes, Scenes will play until the end of the longest Clip, then the next Scene in the Song will be launched.
  • Enabling Loop will relaunch the currently playing Scene
  • If you create a Track named STRUCTURE, any Clip on it will impose its length to the Scene.

docs.title: Arrangement Playback
docs.text:
• For Arrangement Markers, the Arrangement will play until a Marker named STOP is reached
  • Markers between a Song start marker and a STOP marker are ingored, unless they are part of the Setlist, in which case they will be considered as Sections.
  • Enabling Loop will position the Arrangment Loop around the current Section.

docs.title: Using the controller
docs.text:
[CONTROLLER MOCKUP]

  • Press and hold the encoder, and select Setlist
  • Use the buttons to start, stop playback, skip to previous or next Song.
  • Loop button works as described above.
  • Press the encoder to select a Song
  • Press the encoder while holding Shift to select a Setlist

You can send Program Change #5 to the controller (to the DAW port or on External MIDI) to remotely switch to the Setlist page.

docs.title: Using the Performance tab
docs.text:
[CAPTURE d'ECRAN PERFORMANCE]

  • The buttons are the same as above.
  • You can MIDI map the buttons to a MIDI controller by clicking on MIDI Learn.

docs.title: If setlist behavior is wrong
docs.text: Go to Troubleshooting.

docs.title: Defining User actions
docs.text:
1. In the Application, select the User tab.
  2. Select one of the 4 User pages with the Page selector, the controls for the current Page will automatically appear.

  3. For each User page, you can set :

    • Primary Button actions
    • Shifted actions (by clicking on the Shift button ; a blue frame will appear around the controls).
    • Encoder turn/push (Turn is above the encoder, Push is below).
    • Pedal actions
    • Display lines

Actions are sent directly to the controller on select

You can Export and Import page configurations as Json Files.

docs.title: Display Options
docs.text:
These are the 2 lines that appear on the main Display.

Display Type Description Scene Name Current scene/cue name Next Scene Name Upcoming scene name Track Name Selected track name Looper Number Active looper index Variation Number Current variation Prev/Next Marker Name Marker navigation info Setlist Song Current song in setlist Next Setlist Song Upcoming song

docs.title: Buttons Actions
docs.text: These actions are available for the main 5 Buttons, the Pedals and the Encoder when pushing it.

docs.title: Transport Controls
docs.text: Action Description Play/Stop Toggle playback Play Start playback Stop Stop playback Record Toggle recording Metronome Toggle click track BPM +1 / BPM -1 Tempo adjustment Rewind / Fast Forward Timeline navigation

docs.title: Arrangement Controls
docs.text: Action Description Jump 1.1.1 Jump to start Arr. Rec Arrangement recording Arr. Loop Toggle arrangement loop Prev/Next Marker Navigate markers Loop to Next Marker Set loop to next marker

docs.title: Session Controls
docs.text: Action Description Session Rec Session recording Prev/Next Scene Scene navigation Launch Scene Trigger current scene Prev/Next Section Section navigation Loop Section Loop current section

docs.title: Looper Controls
docs.text: Action Description Looper State Toggle looper Prev/Next Looper Navigate loopers Clear Clear current looper Clear All Clear all loopers

docs.title: Custom MIDI
docs.text: Action Description Custom MIDI Send custom Note/CC/PC message

docs.title: Encoder Turn Actions
docs.text: Action Description BPM Adjust tempo Scene Select Navigate scenes Track Select Navigate tracks Scroll General scrolling Selected Parameter Adjust selected parameter Track Volume/Pan Mix controls Track Send A/B Send levels

docs.title: If control behavior mismatches
docs.text: Go to Troubleshooting.
